Trunk port on SW-ACCESS-22 carrying VLANs 10 20 30 — VLAN 30 traffic missing after patch cable was swapped last night. VLANs 10 and 20 working fine. No config changes were made during the cable replacement at all

Seven users on the third floor marketing segment losing access to cloud SaaS apps every afternoon — local intranet resources stay up. Started Monday after BGP policy was adjusted on edge router. Rollback not yet approved by change board

OSPF virtual link between area 5 and backbone not establishing on RTR-AREA5-01 — logs show "dead timer expired" for virtual link neighbor 10.200.200.1. Transit area 2 OSPF is stable. Checked virtual link config — transit area and neighbor ID confirmed correct on both ends

Packet loss of 8 percent on uplink between SW-ACCESS-18 and SW-DIST-04 detected via NetFlow analysis — loss occurring specifically during 1300-1400 window. Interface not saturated — peak utilization 42 percent. Input errors climbing slowly. Transceiver health check not yet performed. Five users on that access switch affected

Spanning tree port role for Gi0/1 on SW-CORE-04 showing "alternate" unexpectedly — should be designated for that segment. Root bridge is correct at SW-CORE-01. Checked port cost — looks correct. No new switches recently added to that segment. TCN count elevated on that port

PPPoE client on router CPE-OFFICE-07 negotiating MTU of 1492 correctly but large packets from internal hosts not being fragmented — TCP MSS clamping not configured on dialer interface. Four users experiencing broken downloads and HTTPS page loading issues for large pages

NAT reflection broken for our internal mail server at 10.0.50.10 — internal clients cannot reach it via public MX record IP 198.51.100.88. Direct internal IP works. DNS internal view not configured for that domain. Five email clients affected when using autodiscover

QoS egress shaping policy on WAN interface of RTR-HUB-01 misconfigured — total shaped rate set to 10 Mbps instead of 100 Mbps for the new fiber circuit installed last week. Eight remote sites experiencing congestion. Policy change in CAB review until tomorrow

text: Juniper EX9208 core switch in chassis slot 3 experiencing continuous FPC restarts — "MQSS_CELL_ERROR: uncorrectable memory error" every 4-7 minutes causing VLAN 1-500 instability. 5

firewall cluster completely lost it this morning after upgrade — blocking internal east-west traffic it never touched before
complexity: letting weird stuff through on the perimeter. 8
domain: 100 users and 400 servers affected across three VLANs. We tried rolling back the firmware but the passive node won't accept the downgrade package. TAC engaged 2 hours ago still waiting

text: Production spanning tree meltdown — 2

complexity: 576 ARP entries reached after cloud migration added 11
